message:
fix(security): comprehensive audit fixes across 8 subsystems

Multi-agent deep audit reviewed ~50 files. Fixes 13 confirmed bugs:

Security (MEDIUM):
- Multipart content_type CRLF injection: sanitize CR/LF/NUL in file part content-type
- HTTP header NUL byte acceptance: reject control chars per RFC 9110 (decode+encode paths)
- SCRAM timing side-channel: use constant-time comparison for server signature verification
- ParsedUrl userinfo rejection: prevent host header confusion from user@host URLs
- Cross-origin redirect credential stripping: remove Authorization/Cookie on origin change

Protocol correctness (MEDIUM):
- gRPC Metadata::get() case-insensitive: use eq_ignore_ascii_case per HTTP/2 semantics
- CORS preflight Vary header: use append_vary_header instead of clobbering .insert()
- PostgreSQL bind message overflow: i32::try_from() guard on parameter value length
- PgTransaction Drop: set needs_rollback flag, auto-ROLLBACK before next query/execute
- HTTP extension method validation: add is_valid_token() filter per RFC 9110

Reliability (MEDIUM):
- Actor on_stop cancellation masking: mask cancel during cleanup (matching GenServer)
- Process wait_async: exponential backoff (1ms→50ms) instead of busy-loop yield_now

Protocol completeness (LOW):
- Add 11 missing HTTP status reason phrases (101, 202, 303, 307, 308, 409, 410, etc.)
- NUL byte filtering in escape_content_disposition_value

Modules found sound (0 bugs): sync primitives (mutex/rwlock/pool), I/O extensions,
browser streams, TLS acceptor, lab runtime, HTTP/2 settings, happy eyeballs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
